title: Oncocyclus ibericus
intro: The Oncocyclus ibericus, also known as the Spanish broom or French broom, is a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its long, slender branches and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Oncocyclus ibericus include isoflavones, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
taxonomy: Oncocyclus ibericus is a species within the genus Oncocyclus. The Oncocyclus genus belongs to the subtribe Orchidinae, which is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Further classification includes the family Orchidaceae, which is a part of the order Asparagales, a clade within the monocots. The class is Liliopsida, which is a part of the larger group of flowering plants, Magnoliophyta. Ultimately, Oncocyclus ibericus is classified as a flowering plant within the kingdom Plantae.
